framework_selection.selection_reason: Fictional Japanese listed company with an integrated report and sustainability report; use Japan core plus global comparability checks.

scoring_audit.report_boundary_policy: fictional integrated report plus fictional sustainability report
audit_notes: Horizontal tag calibration completed across all nine tags.
Same 0-10 score scale and cap rules applied to every tag.
Fictional sample only; report URLs intentionally point to example.com placeholders.

top_strengths: Clear value creation narrative tied to customer problems, process technology, and service revenue.
Human capital and intangible asset initiatives are connected to strategy.
Several KPIs include time-series progress and board-level accountability.
top_gaps: Capital allocation link to non-financial value drivers is still partial.
Materiality lacks quantified financial impact ranges.
Climate and supply-chain risk exposure is not scenario quantified.
comparability_warnings: Fictional data is designed for UI demonstration, not company assessment.
Report boundary differs from peer sustainability reports.

report_sources.selection_reason: Fictional official company PDF from report library.
Fictional official company PDF from sustainability archive.

scores.label: Value creation story
scores.definition: Whether the company explains how business model, strategy, resources, stakeholder relationships, and outcomes connect as a causal story.
scores.reason: Narrative explains the path from customer productivity issues to strategic assets, process technology, employee skills, and recurring service revenue, but downside trade-offs are limited.
missing_evidence: More explicit negative-outcome and trade-off discussion.
score_caps_applied: No explicit negative/tradeoff evidence: score not raised above 8.

scores.label: Materiality
scores.definition: Whether important value drivers and sustainability-related issues are identified, prioritized, and linked to enterprise value.
scores.reason: Material themes are ranked and linked to strategy, but financial effect ranges and refresh ownership are not disclosed.
missing_evidence: Financial effect range by material topic.

scores.label: Business model and outcomes
scores.definition: Whether inputs, business activities, outputs, outcomes, and key stakeholders are described in a connected model.
scores.reason: Business model shows inputs, process technology, service model, customer outcomes, and recurring revenue, with partial supplier and community outcome disclosure.
missing_evidence: Outcome metrics for suppliers and communities.
score_caps_applied: Stakeholder outcome metrics are partial: cap 8.
calibration_notes: Kept below high anchor because outcome data is incomplete.

scores.label: Non-financial capital
scores.definition: Whether human, intellectual, social, natural, and other non-financial capitals are formed, used, and protected.
scores.reason: Human capital, process patents, customer community, and energy-use reduction are described as strategic assets, but capital impairment and return-on-capital logic are thin.
calibration_notes: Evidence supports a good but not advanced SIX score.

scores.label: Strategy and resource allocation
scores.definition: Whether resources and investments are allocated to material issues and business-model priorities.
scores.reason: Strategy names automation, service expansion, and energy-efficiency investments, but capital allocation thresholds and scenario triggers are not explicit.
calibration_notes: Lowered below KPI because the roadmap lacks decision rules.

scores.label: KPI outcomes
scores.definition: Whether targets, actual results, time series, and value-driver links are disclosed.
scores.reason: KPI table includes employee engagement, service revenue, energy intensity, and customer retention, but the hierarchy to enterprise value is incomplete.
missing_evidence: Longer time series for newer targets.
calibration_notes: KPI score kept near MAT due to similar evidence depth.

scores.label: Governance and accountability
scores.definition: Whether the board and management oversee non-financial value creation, risks, incentives, and accountability.
scores.reason: Board committee oversight and executive scorecard links are visible, but board deliberation examples and corrective actions remain summarized.
calibration_notes: Governance is stronger than STR but below advanced.

scores.label: Risks and opportunities
scores.definition: Whether climate, nature, human rights, supply chain, technology, and regulation are linked to future value.
scores.reason: Climate transition, supply-chain resilience, and human-rights due diligence are identified, but exposure quantification and scenario financial impacts are missing.
calibration_notes: Lowest tag because risk disclosure is mostly qualitative.

scores.label: Disclosure quality
scores.definition: Whether disclosure is concrete, comparable, evidence-based, and balanced between positive and negative information.
scores.reason: Disclosure is structured, source-linked, and evidence-backed, but it uses fictional placeholders and still lacks full external assurance coverage.
missing_evidence: External assurance over all non-financial KPIs.
calibration_notes: Disclosure quality reflects traceability but not full assurance.

evidence.excerpt: The company links customer productivity issues to proprietary process technology, employee skills, and recurring service revenue.
Inputs include process engineers, patents, supplier know-how, and customer data; outcomes include reduced downtime and higher lifecycle service attachment.
The report identifies climate transition, human capital, and supply-chain resilience as priority issues with medium-term targets.
Management plans to increase automation R&D and service platform investment while reducing exposure to low-margin equipment lines.
KPIs include employee engagement, safety incidents, energy intensity, customer retention, and service revenue mix for fiscal years 2024-2026.
The board sustainability committee reviews materiality progress quarterly, and 15% of executive variable compensation is linked to non-financial targets.
The report lists transition risk, supplier concentration, and carbon-price exposure, but does not quantify financial sensitivity.
Supplier self-assessments cover forced labor and safety risks; remediation status is summarized by region without supplier-level exposure.
